entries.name: FirebaseFunctionMapKey
entries.notes: String key used to identify a function group in the app-level functions map.

entries.name: FirebaseFunctionKey
entries.notes: Key that corresponds with a function on the server.

entries.name: ModelFirebaseCrudFunctionSpecifier
entries.notes:
String identifier that routes a CRUD request to a specific sub-handler within a model's
CRUD function. For example, a model's `update` might have specifiers like `'status'` or `'config'`
to target different update behaviors.

entries.name: FirebaseAuthUserId
entries.notes: Unique identifier for a Firebase Authentication user (the `uid` from Firebase Auth).

entries.name: FirebaseAuthIdToken
entries.notes:
Raw encoded JWT for Firebase Auth.

Corresponds with IdToken type in Firebase Auth.

entries.name: FirebaseAuthOwnershipKey
entries.notes:
A string key used to test for ownership of a particular set of objects.

For instance, a database model (or its parent) may have an ownership key associated with it that is compared with the user's current claims.

entries.name: DevelopmentFirebaseFunctionSpecifier
entries.notes:
Identifies a specific development function endpoint to route requests to.

Used in dev/test environments to invoke specific server-side functions by name.

entries.name: StorageBucketId
entries.notes:
Storage bucket identifier for Firebase Cloud Storage.

Should contain no slashes — just the bucket name (e.g., `"my-app.appspot.com"`).

entries.name: StorageDownloadUrl
entries.notes: The public storage url link.

entries.name: StorageSignedDownloadUrl
entries.notes: A signed download link.

entries.name: StorageRawDataString
entries.notes:
Example:
'Hello! \\ud83d\\ude0a';

entries.name: StorageBase64DataString
entries.notes:
Example:
'5b6p5Y+344GX44G+44GX44Gf77yB44GK44KB44Gn44Go44GG77yB';

entries.name: StorageBase64UrlDataString
entries.notes:
Example:
'5b6p5Y-344GX44G-44GX44Gf77yB44GK44KB44Gn44Go44GG77yB';

entries.name: StorageDataUrlString
entries.notes:
Example:
'data:text/plain;base64,5b6p5Y+344GX44G+44GX44Gf77yB44GK44KB44Gn44Go44GG77yB'

entries.name: NotificationTemplateType
entries.notes:
Template type identifier of the notification.

Provides default information for the notification.

Types are generally intended to be handled case-insensitively by notification services.

Ideally type values are shorter to reduce database size impact.

entries.name: NotificationTaskType
entries.notes:
Task type identifier of the notification, which is used to pass this task to the appropriate handler.

Ideally type values are shorter to reduce database size impact.

entries.name: NotificationMessageEntityKey
entries.notes:
Arbitrary key used by the sending configuration service for choosing a pre-configured entity.

Typically used for customizing the "from" or "replyTo" addresses while maintaining a separation of concerns.

entries.name: NotificationSendMessageTemplateName
entries.notes: Arbitrary template name/key that is used to configure which template to use by the sending service.

entries.name: NotificationTaskSubtaskTarget
entries.notes: Discriminator string that routes the subtask to the correct processing configuration.

entries.name: NotificationTaskCheckpointString
entries.notes: Arbitrary unique string that denotes checkpoint progress for a multi-step task.

entries.name: OidcEntryClientId
entries.notes: Unique client identifier for an OIDC client registration.

entries.name: OidcInteractionUid
entries.notes:
Unique identifier for an oidc-provider interaction session, not to be confused with a Firebase UID/Auth User Identifier.

Generated by the provider when user interaction is needed (login/consent).
Used to look up the interaction session via cookies and to build
backend interaction endpoint URLs (e.g., `/interaction/${uid}/login`).

entries.name: OidcScope
entries.notes:
Base type for OIDC scope string unions.

Applications define their own scope union extending this type to get
compile-time validation of scope names throughout the delegate and config.

entries.name: OidcRedirectUri
entries.notes:
A redirect URI registered to an OIDC client.

Must be a valid absolute URL (e.g. `https://myapp.example.com/callback`).

entries.name: StorageFilePurpose
entries.notes:
Arbitrary string that can be used to describe the file's purpose.

It should be unique between different file types.

The purpose is generally used while querying for StorageFiles that require processing, but can also be
set on files that do not get processed.

Can be used for querying.

entries.name: StorageFilePurposeSubgroup
entries.notes:
Used as an arbitrary discriminator for a StorageFile in relation to the StorageFilePurpose.

This is useful for being able to query StorageFiles that have a specific purpose and purpose subgroup.

Example use case: Documents with the same StorageFilePurpose/processing, but should only have a single StorageFile per subgroup.

entries.name: UploadedFileTypeDeterminationLevel
entries.notes:
Confidence level of a file type determination. Higher values indicate higher confidence.

When multiple determiners match, the one with the highest level wins.

Higher values indicate higher confidence.

For example, a match by a file name would potentially have a higher confidence than a match by folder name
in the case where we have two determiners:
- One looking for 'avatar.png' exactly
- One looking for the folder 'photos' exactly

In this case, the folder match should return a lower determination level than the file name match, so that
it is processed properly.

In the ideal case there generally shouldn't be two determiners that could potentially match the same file.

entries.name: UploadedFileTypeIdentifier
entries.notes:
StorageFile uploaded-file type identifier.

Used as a descriminator for choosing the appropriate upload processor.

The upload type is generally determined by one of a few ways:
- file name: A specific file name (e.g. 'avatar.png' or 'photos/avatar.png')
- folder name: A specific folder name (e.g. 'photos' in 'photos/12345.png')
- metadata: specific metadata value in the uploaded file's custom metadata
- data: specific data in the uploaded file

entries.name: SystemStateTypeIdentifier
entries.notes: Used to identify a SystemStateId.

entries.name: SingleItemFirestoreCollectionDocumentIdentifier
entries.notes: Document ID used for the single document in a single-item Firestore collection.

entries.name: FirestoreCollectionName
entries.notes:
An all lowercase name that references a collection. Is usually the lowercase version of the FirestoreModelType.

This is the part of the path that identifies what the collection is. Collection names are used in
Firestore paths and should follow Firestore naming conventions (lowercase, no spaces, no special characters, since "_" is reserved/used for TwoWayFlatFirestoreModelKey).

Each collection name in the app should be unique, as usage of CollectionGroups would cause collections
with the same name to be returned regardless of their location in the document hierarchy.

entries.name: FirestoreModelId
entries.notes:
The model's id within a collection.

Different from the FirestoreModelKey, which is the full path in the databse.

Example:

12345

entries.name: FirestoreModelCollectionTypeArrayName
entries.notes:
String that is composed of the FirestoreCollectionNames derived from an input FirestoreModelKey and joined together via a separator.

Is equivalent to a FirestoreCollectionType if the FIRESTORE_COLLECTION_NAME_SEPARATOR is used as the separator.

entries.name: FirestoreDriverIdentifier
entries.notes: Optional human-readable identifier for the Firestore driver (e.g., 'firebase-admin', 'firebase-web', 'testing').

entries.name: FirestoreQueryConstraintType
entries.notes:
Type identifier for a Firestore query constraint.

Used to uniquely identify different types of query constraints, such as
where, limit, orderBy, etc.

entries.name: StorageListFilesPageToken
entries.notes: String used as a cursor for iterating pages of file results.

entries.name: StorageListFileResultNextPageToken
entries.notes: Token returned with a list-files result for resuming pagination on the next call.

entries.name: FirebaseStorageDriverIdentifier
entries.notes: Unique identifier string for a storage driver implementation.
